situation: Korean student applied for a 2-year Master's program in Germany. Set up a Fintiba blocked account with €10,500, which was the correct amount for 2024. The 2026 minimum had increased to €11,208 per year. Visa was denied without prior notification of the shortfall.
rejection_reason: Blocked account balance of €10,500 did not meet the 2026 minimum requirement of €11,208. Embassy denied the application citing §16 AufenthG for insufficient proof of financial subsistence.
what_worked: Topped up the Fintiba account by €708 to reach €11,208. Obtained an updated Fintiba certificate. Reapplied within 2 weeks. Visa approved on the second application. Added a note to check for annual updates to the required amount every January.
what_failed: Appealing the decision arguing the original amount was correct when the account was opened. Providing scholarship offer letters as supplementary financial proof. Submitting parental income statements to cover the gap.
